📝 Unofficial summary – not affiliated with the creator.

00:00 - Introduction:
The speaker introduces a simple yet effective day trading strategy used daily for consistent profits.
The strategy sets up within the first 90 minutes of market open.
It is rules-based, requiring no bias or fancy indicators.
The video will cover the full strategy, a 3-step checklist, and real trading examples.
00:32 - Proof of Concept & Announcement:
Shows last 30 days P&L, making over $90,000.
Announces a free event on September 13th teaching how to become a profitable trader from scratch.
01:35 - Strategy Overview:
Goal: Learn a simple, proven strategy for consistent profits within 90 minutes of market open.
Features: Repeatable, mechanical, no bias needed, no fancy indicators.
Components: 3 simple steps, 2 entry models, entries/stops/targets, live examples.
02:44 - Step 1: Wait for New York Open:
Wait for the market to open at 9:30 AM EST.
New York session is chosen for its high volume and volatility, ideal for stocks and indices.
03:42 - Step 2: Identify 5-Minute Opening Range:
Focus on the first 5-minute candle of the session.
Mark the high and low of this first candle. This defines the "5-minute opening range".
04:55 - Step 3: Look for Breakout (Entry Model 1 - Retest):
Switch to the 1-minute timeframe.
Wait for a candle to close above or below the 5-minute range.
Look for a retest of the range for entry.
Entry: On the retest of the 5-minute range level.
Stop Loss: Just below/above the range break level (or a wider stop for higher timeframe thesis).
Target: Minimum 1:2 Risk/Reward ratio.
06:59 - Entry Model 2 - Breakout with Displacement:
Used when price doesn't retest or for a more aggressive entry.
Wait for a break above/below the 5-minute range with displacement on the 1-minute chart.
Displacement: Identified by a Fair Value Gap (FVG) - a gap between the 1st candle's high/low and the 3rd candle's low/high in a 3-candle sequence.
Entry: On the close of the 3rd candle that confirms the displacement.
Stop Loss: Similar to the retest model, below/above the range or breakout candle.
Target: Minimum 1:2 Risk/Reward ratio.
09:50 - Timeframe Selection (5-min vs 15-min):
Use 5-minute opening range for higher timeframe thesis or expected volatility.
Use 15-minute opening range if the market is ranging.
10:18 - Example 1: NVDA (Short):
Context: Downtrend, below previous day lows.
Setup: 5-minute opening range marked. Price breaks below.
Entry: Retest of the 5-minute range low.
Outcome: Rejection and move lower, hitting 1:2 target. Net profit: ~$14,000.
14:26 - Example 2: AMD (Long):
Context: Earnings reversal, higher timeframe thesis.
Setup: 5-minute opening range marked. Price breaks above.
Entry: Retest of the 5-minute range high.
Outcome: Bounce and continuation higher. Net profit: ~$19,000.
18:12 - Example 3: TSLA (Short):
Context: Downtrend, below previous day lows.
Setup: 5-minute opening range marked. Price breaks below.
Entry: Retest of the 5-minute range low.
Outcome: Rejection and continuation lower. Net profit: ~$8,000.
19:59 - Conclusion:
Recap of the strategy's effectiveness in capturing trend moves.
Reminder about the upcoming free event and waitlist link.


⚠ Disclaimer: For educational purposes only. I am not the creator, not a financial advisor, and this is not trading or investment advice. Trading involves substantial risk — always do your own research and never risk capital you can’t afford to lose.
